questions.question: What does the length() method of the String class return?
options: The number of words in the string
The number of characters in the string
The number of bytes in the string
The index of the last character
questions.answer: The number of characters in the string

questions.question: What is the return type of the charAt() method?
What does indexOf(String s) return if the substring is not found?
What is the result of "Hello, World!".substring(7, 12)?
Why do String methods like toUpperCase() return a new String instead of modifying the original?
options: Because Strings in Java are immutable
Because Java does not support in-place modification of any object
Because toUpperCase() is inherited from the Object class
questions.answer: Because Strings in Java are immutable

questions.question: What does the trim() method remove?
options: All whitespace characters in the string

Why does method chaining like str.trim().toUpperCase() work with Strings?
options: Because String methods modify the original string and return void
Because each String method returns a new String, which can have another method called on it
Because Java automatically converts method calls to loops
Because String is a primitive type that supports chaining
questions.answer: Because each String method returns a new String, which can have another method called on it
